summary: Adversarial verification of every claim group in the Ep3 'Food' script (draft of 2026-07-01). 12 claim groups, one Fable-max skeptic each, instructed to refute against live primary sources. July 20 amendment: a SUN Bucks (Summer EBT) guest beat (Clay) and the corrected Community Roots summer-meals attribution were added and verified against primary sources -- see the 'sunbucks' and 'communityroots-summermeals' entries.
logs: 12/12 verified; 6 accurate, 6 needs_fix; ALL fixes applied to ep3-teleprompter.txt on 2026-07-01
2 agents were respawned once after a transient API connection error (no output lost)
No spoken dollar figure or eligibility rule was wrong; every fix trims an overpromise, prevents staleness, or upgrades a program card

fixDecisions.cf-hr1: Applied the FULL rewrite (not the minimum insert): CFAP is no longer implied as the refugee safety net; public-charge reassurance + legal-aid CTA kept; closes on 'the food bank never asks about status'.
fixDecisions.marketmatch: Applied Option B (producer-preferred): definite matched-dollars promise cut; confirmed-EBT line + ask-at-the-booth hedge aired instead. Shawna's setup line and the closing recap echo were also rewritten. RESTORE PATH: if Sierra Harvest / county DSS confirm a live 2026-27 cycle before July 20, restore the original beat below with the cap they state.
fixDecisions.marketmatch_originalBeat: My favorite trick in this episode. It's called Market Match. At the Nevada City farmers market on Saturdays, take your CalFresh card to the market manager's booth. Whatever you spend there, they match it -- up to ten extra dollars every visit, free -- to spend on fresh fruits and vegetables. Grocery money that literally grows at the market.
fixDecisions.ifm: Applied full rewrite: drive-through reality replaces 'choice pantry / pick your own groceries'; 10-11am reservations-only window now stated; card notes first hour by reservation.
fixDecisions.seniors-meals: Applied Truckee eligibility hedge (delivery is for mostly-homebound; group-meal fallback offered); card upgraded with full center name + lunch-cafe phone 530-273-4961.
fixDecisions.cf-apply: Spoken line now offers Nevada City OR Truckee; card adds the Truckee Joseph Center office and local 530-265-1340; 888-809-1340 is banned (not on any live county page).
fixDecisions.fbnc: Summer Lunch reframed evergreen ('Every summer... this year through the end of July... current sites at foodbankofnc dot org') so YouTube replays stay true.
microFixesAdopted: 'each kid ages one to five' (infants get formula/baby food, not the produce benefit)
fixDecisions.optionalPolishesNotApplied: Host-discretion ad-lib softeners recorded in each verdict's scriptFix field (cf-ssi-seniors, wic-eligibility, wic-apply, wic-benefits) -- put on cue cards, not in prompter.

hostAdLibBlacklist: Never say veterans, former foster youth, or homeless folks are exempt from the CalFresh work rule -- H.R.1 eliminated those exemptions
Never imply refugees/asylees can switch to CFAP -- they are not CFAP-eligible as of July 2026
Never say undocumented adults 55+ can get CFAP now -- that expansion is delayed to 2027-28
Never promise Market Match dollars as a sure thing -- matching is OFF at Nevada City as of July 1
Never quote WIC produce money for infants under 1 -- they get formula/baby food instead
Never improvise new dollar figures -- every number spoken on air must be one of the scripted, verified ones

weekOfRecheck.note: Run the standard week-of pass (July 14-18). Every remaining confirm is cued inline in the txt; the one ACTION item is the Market Match call (Sierra Harvest / county DSS).
pending: SUN Bucks: $120/child + Aug 31 2026 application deadline still posted on cdss.ca.gov/sun-bucks (added Jul 20)

Community Roots summer-meal site list + Aug 7 library end date; NOTE Nevada Union HS pickup ends Jul 30 -- drop from the spoken line if airing after (communityrootsnc.org / library 530-265-7050) (added Jul 20)
Market Match 2026-27 funding (ACTION: phone call; restore original beat if confirmed)
CDSS H.R.1 FAQ re-check (work rules + immigrant eligibility)
FBNC July distribution dates + summer-lunch sites/end date + ~2,500 stat still posted
WIC Truckee phone 7814 vs 7817 at the pre-record call to 530-265-1454
USDA/CDSS dollar figures (routine confirm only -- verified vs primary PDF July 1)

result.notes: VERIFIED 2026-07-20 vs primary sources for the SUN Bucks (Summer EBT) beat added in the July 20 amendment (guest: Clay). $120 per eligible child for summer 2026 -- the Governor's launch (gov.ca.gov/2026/05/21/california-launches-sun-bucks-for-summer-2026) states '$40 per month for food in June, July, and August ($120 total) per eligible child' and 'More than 3.5 million eligible children in California will be automatically enrolled'; cdss.ca.gov/sun-bucks confirms '$120 per eligible child' and helpline (877) 328-9677. Auto-enroll: free/reduced-price school meals OR CalFresh/CalWORKs/Medi-Cal (<=185% FPL) OR school-identified foster/homeless/migrant; auto cards mailed late May. Not-auto-enrolled families apply via a school-meal application or the Universal Benefits Application to the child's school BY AUGUST 31, 2026. Spends like CalFresh at grocery stores. Script states $120/child, the auto-enroll paths, 'on top of' the summer meal sites, and the Aug 31 deadline -- all match. BURN RISK: do not imply SUN Bucks replaces the summer meal sites; it stacks (script says 'use both'). WEEK-OF RECHECK: re-confirm $120 + the Aug 31 deadline on cdss.ca.gov/sun-bucks before the July 27 record.

VERIFIED 2026-07-20 vs communityrootsnc.org/summer-meals (Community Roots = Nevada County School Food Services JPA) + the county library page. CORRECTS a v1 mis-attribution: the kids' summer MEALS program is run by COMMUNITY ROOTS via the USDA Summer Food Service Program (the library 'Lunch at the Library' is theirs), NOT the food bank as the v1 draft stated -- Shawna flagged this. Verified, all free, any child 18 & under, no sign-up/paperwork/income test: daily lunch 12-1 at Grass Valley Library (207 Mill St) + Madelyn Helling Library (980 Helling Way, Nevada City) + Memorial Park Pool (350 Race St, GV), June 22-Aug 7 (pool from June 15); Scotten School (821 Gilmore Way, times TBD, thru Aug 12); weekly meal bags (7 breakfasts + 7 lunches) Thursdays 10-1 at Nevada Union High School (11761 Ridge Rd, GV, June 18-JULY 30) + Oak Tree Preschool (18847 Oaktree Rd, Nevada City, June 18-Aug 13). Script keeps it evergreen ('right into August' / 'full list at communityrootsnc dot org') -- no stale dates locked in speech. The food bank stays in the script for its GROCERY distributions (accurate). TIMING BURN RISK: Nevada Union HS weekly pickup ENDS July 30 -- drop that one site from the spoken line if the episode airs after July 30. WEEK-OF RECHECK: re-confirm the site list + the Aug 7 library end date before the July 27 record (communityrootsnc.org or library 530-265-7050).

PRIMARY PDF RETRIEVED AND EYEBALLED. Live usda.gov/fns.usda.gov fetches still fail from this environment (HTTP/2 INTERNAL_ERROR, then timeout on --http1.1), so the memo was pulled via archive.org: snapshot 2026-04-13 of https://www.usda.gov/sites/default/files/guidance-documents/fns.snap-cola-fy26memo.pdf (original bytes via web.archive.org/web/20260413163948id_/..., 1,583,795 bytes, valid PDF; saved locally at scratch/ep3-food/factcheck/fns-snap-cola-fy26memo.pdf with pdftotext extraction alongside). Memo is titled 'SNAP – Fiscal Year 2026 Cost-of-Living Adjustments', COLAs effective October 1, 2025. Confirmed in the memo: (1) Maximum SNAP Allotments table for 48 States and D.C., 'OCTOBER 1, 2025, TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2026' — HH1 = $298, HH4 = $994; body text also states 'The maximum allotment for a family of four in the 48 States and D.C., will be $994' and 'The minimum benefit for the 48 States and D.C. will increase to $24.' (2) MINIMUM SNAP ALLOTMENTS table has exactly one row, household size '1–2' = $24 (48 States and D.C.) — confirming the minimum applies only to 1- and 2-person households. CROSS-CHECK: archived FNS COLA page https://www.fns.usda.gov/snap/allotment/COLA (wayback snapshot 2026-06-05, i.e., ~7 weeks before record date) shows identical FY2026 tables: HH1 $298 / HH4 $994 max, minimum '1–2' $24. Two independent captures (Apr + Jun 2026) agree, so figures are stable through the fiscal year and valid at the July 20 record and late-July air dates (FY2026 runs through Sept 30, 2026). 'Most folks land somewhere in between' is consistent with the SNAP benefit formula (allotment = max − 30% of net income, 7 CFR 273.10(e)(2)): any household with positive net income receives less than the max, and only 1-2 person households have the $24 floor — 'most' is a fair, non-overstated characterization. Script wording matches sources exactly. RESIDUAL RISK (low): figures re-verified against a June 5 2026 capture, not a week-of-record capture; H.R.1 could in principle force a mid-fiscal-year change, but none had occurred as of the June snapshot — the brief's existing 're-verify week of July 20' flag covers this.

Every scripted statement survived adversarial checking against LIVE primary sources (verified 2026-07-01; nevadacountyca.gov 403 was UA-blocking — full live pages retrieved via curl with a browser user-agent, no archive.org needed). (1) BenefitsCal CONFIRMED: calfresh.dss.ca.gov/food/ lists exactly three application methods — 'Online: Benefitscal.com', 'By Phone: 877 847 3663', 'In Person'; benefitscal.com is live ('BenefitsCal. Together, we benefit.') and the county's own CalFresh page directs applicants there; CalFresh has no enrollment window, so 'any day of the year' holds. (2) 1-877-847-3663 CONFIRMED current on calfresh.dss.ca.gov. (3) County office CONFIRMED on live nevadacountyca.gov/892 and /903 + facility pages: 950 Maidu Avenue, Suite 120, Nevada City, CA 95959 (Eric Rood Administration Center), M-F 8am-5pm, phone 530-265-1340, fax 530-265-9860. ZIP 'discrepancy' resolved — 95949 is the MAIL-application P.O. Box (P.O. Box 1210, Nevada City, CA 95949, per county page); the physical office is 95959; card uses the physical address, so no error. The 'Nevada City Courthouse, 201 Church St' walk-in site appears on NO live county page — stale third-party listing, do NOT add. ⚠️ 888-809-1340 appears on NO live county page (checked all 5 pages); only 530-265-1340 is published — do not put the 888 number on the card. NEW FINDING: the county operates THREE full-service public assistance offices ('you can apply for benefits... at any of the three'): Nevada City (950 Maidu Ave Ste 120), Grass Valley (Brighton Greens Resource Center, 988 McCourtney Rd, 95949, M-F 8-5), and TRUCKEE (Joseph Center, 10075 Levon Ave, Truckee 96161, M-F 8am-5pm) — main phone for all three is 530-265-1340. Truckee SHOULD be on the card for east-county viewers. (4) Expedited CONFIRMED verbatim from live CDSS CF285 PDF + county page: 'benefits within 3 calendar days' if gross monthly income <$150 AND cash/checking/savings ≤$100, OR housing costs (rent/mortgage + utilities) exceed gross income + money on hand, OR migrant/seasonal farmworker w/ <$100; 'almost no money and no food' is a fair colloquial rendering, and 'say so' is correct advice (counties must screen for expedited service). (5) Renewal CONFIRMED: 12-month certification + SAR-7 semi-annual report at month 6 (CDSS SAR guidance; county page has its own 'How to Complete the SAR 7' section) — supports 'not forever-automatic: answer the county's renewal mail.' Bonus (optional, verify week-of): 211/Connecting Point lists monthly drop-in CalFresh application help at Grass Valley Library (1st Tuesdays 10:30am-12:30pm) and Madelyn Helling Library (1st Wednesdays 10am-12pm).
result.scriptFix: Script wording can stand as-is (nothing stated is false). Two card fixes: (a) ADD the Truckee office for east-county viewers — card line: 'Nevada County offices: 950 Maidu Ave, Suite 120, Nevada City · 10075 Levon Ave, Truckee (Joseph Center) · 530-265-1340' (Grass Valley's Brighton Greens, 988 McCourtney Rd, optional third). Optionally have the host say 'county offices in Nevada City, Grass Valley, and Truckee' instead of just Nevada City. (b) Do NOT use 888-809-1340 anywhere — it is not on any current county page; use 530-265-1340 as the local number. Keep BenefitsCal.com, 1-877-847-3663, and 2-1-1 exactly as scripted.

result.notes: VERIFIED 2026-07-01 against live primary sources. CLAIM (a) WORK RULES: ACCURATE. CDSS H.R.1 & CalFresh FAQ (page updated May 29, 2026) confirms every element: rules changed 'Starting June 1, 2026'; apply to ages 18-64; qualifying activity 'up to 20 hours per week, or 80 hours averaged monthly' (work, volunteering, and training all count). Every exemption the script names is live on the CDSS FAQ + ABAWD page as of the fetch: parent/responsible for a dependent child UNDER 14 ('child need not reside with them'), pregnant (any stage), medically unable to work 20+ hrs (physical or mental), caring for a sick/disabled person needing help >30 days, going to school at least half-time. 'And more' is earned: also exempt are disability applicants/recipients, tribal members (IHCIA), ORR trainees, addiction/DV/chronic-homelessness tied to a health condition, and 7 waived counties (Nevada County NOT among them: Alpine, Colusa, Imperial, Merced, Monterey, Plumas, Tulare). H.R.1 ELIMINATED the old categorical exemptions for veterans, former foster youth, homeless individuals, and ages 55-64 — the script wisely names none of these, but hosts MUST NOT ad-lib them. Time limit: 3 countable months per 3 years (statewide 36-month clock Jan 2026-Dec 2028), so 'keep benefits long-term' is fair. CLAIM (b) IMMIGRANTS: THE FACTS ARE RIGHT BUT THE JUXTAPOSITION MISLEADS — THIS IS THE BURN. April 1, 2026 federal loss confirmed (CDSS FAQ): refugees, asylees, parolees (except Cuban/Haitian), withholding-of-deportation, conditional entrants, trafficking victims, battered noncitizens, Iraqi/Afghan SIVs, certain Afghan/Ukrainian parolees; ~72,000 Californians affected (CDSS estimate via Nourish CA 4/9/26 release). CFAP exists and is public-charge-safe (CDSS: 'CalFresh and CFAP are not considered public charge programs'), and CFAP benefits are CalFresh-equivalent amounts for those it covers — so 'same benefit amounts' holds. BUT the live CDSS CFAP who-is-eligible page lists ONLY: LPRs under the 5-year/40-quarter bar, parolees, conditional entrants, and battered/abused noncitizens. REFUGEES AND ASYLEES ARE NOT CFAP-ELIGIBLE. The June 2026 CA budget deal did NOT expand CFAP to backfill the H.R.1 exclusions (LAO 5126/5149; Assembly floor report 6/11/26; Food4All coalition is still ASKING for it, which proves it hasn't happened); the only committed expansion (undocumented 55+) is delayed to Oct 2027. The script's sequence — 'refugees lost FEDERAL CalFresh. But California runs a state version... that still covers some non-citizens' — will be heard by a refugee family as 'the state version covers me.' It does not. AD-LIB BURN RISKS, worst-first: (1) 'refugees can switch to the state program' → refugee family waits for a CFAP approval that never comes, forgoes food bank/legal help = the single worst on-air outcome; (2) 'veterans/homeless folks are exempt from the work rules' → H.R.1 killed those exemptions; viewer skips reporting hours, burns their 3 countable months, locked out up to 3 years; (3) 'undocumented folks 55+ can get state food benefits now' → delayed to Oct 2027; (4) softening the work rules with 'they never really enforce it' → clock IS running since Jan 1, 2026. RE-VERIFY week of July 20: CDSS FAQ page-update date, CFAP who-is-eligible list (advocacy pressure could add groups late), and any trailer-bill action — this is the fastest-moving claim group in the episode. Sources: cdss.ca.gov H.R.1 FAQ (updated 5/29/26); cdss.ca.gov/inforesources/calfresh/abawd; cdss.ca.gov/inforesources/cdss-programs/calfresh/cfap/who-is-eligible; nourishca.org press release 4/9/26; lao.ca.gov Reports 5126 & 5149; abgt.assembly.ca.gov floor report 6/11/26; KQED 12078168.
result.scriptFix: Keep (a) as written — every named exemption verified live; brief hosts to NEVER ad-lib veterans, former foster youth, or homeless as exempt (H.R.1 eliminated those). Rewrite (b) so CFAP is not offered as the refugee safety net: 'As of April, some immigrants who are here lawfully — including refugees — lost FEDERAL CalFresh. California does run a state version, the California Food Assistance Program, with the same benefit amounts — but it only covers certain groups, like green-card holders in their first five years and survivors of abuse. Right now it does NOT cover most refugees who lost the federal benefit. So if anyone in your household isn't a citizen: don't guess, and don't panic. Using food benefits won't hurt a green-card application — but get free advice from a legal-aid nonprofit first. And the food bank never asks about status.' If runtime forces a shorter cut, the minimum viable fix is inserting 'but it only covers certain groups — and right now most refugees aren't among them' after 'California Food Assistance Program', keeping the legal-aid CTA.

result.notes: ADVERSARIAL CHECK — could not refute either claim; every element verified against live primary sources 2026-07-01. (a) CARD MECHANICS: calfresh.dss.ca.gov/food/index.html (CDSS, fetched live) describes CalFresh as 'Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP, formerly Food Stamps)' that 'helps put groceries on the table', delivered via an EBT card that 'works like a debit card and automatically reloads every month', usable 'at most grocery stores, convenience stores, farmers markets, and even online'. Script language is near-verbatim CDSS phrasing — 'used to call food stamps', 'works like a debit card', 'reloads every month', 'spend it on groceries' all confirmed. cdss.ca.gov/inforesources/ebt independently confirms 'much like a debit card' + retailer/farmers-market/online acceptance (Safeway, Amazon, Walmart). (b) INCOME LIMITS: CDSS ACIN I-46-25 (Sept 3, 2025; cdss.ca.gov/Portals/9/Additional-Resources/Letters-and-Notices/ACINs/2025/I-46_25.pdf, Attachment I, read in full) — 'Gross Monthly Income Eligibility Standards for Modified Categorical Eligibility (MCE)/Broad-Based Categorical Eligibility (BBCE) (200% of Poverty Level)', effective OCTOBER 1, 2025 – SEPTEMBER 30, 2026 (covers July 20 record + air date): HH1 = $2,610, HH4 = $5,360. Live calfresh.dss.ca.gov/food/eligibility/index.html shows the same figures ($2,610/$3,526/$4,442/$5,360) labeled 'Gross Monthly Income' = before taxes — script's 'before taxes' is correct. Script's spoken 'about twenty-six hundred' (vs $2,610) and 'about fifty-three hundred' (vs $5,360) both UNDERSTATE slightly — conservative direction, acceptable. 'TWICE the federal poverty line' = 200% FPL, exact. 'Most households' hedge is required and present: MCE/BBCE 200% doesn't cover everyone — households where elderly/disabled are a separate household use a 165% gross standard for the others ($2,152 HH1 / $4,421 HH4 per same ACIN); passing the 200% gross screen still leaves the net-income test (100% FPL: $1,305 HH1 / $2,680 HH4) driving the actual benefit, so a household near the gross cap may get little or nothing. Do NOT strengthen 'most' to 'all' or drop 'about'. CDSS H.R.1 FAQ (cdss.ca.gov, fetched live) confirms the 2026 H.R.1 changes (utility deduction Nov 2025, immigrant eligibility Apr 2026, work rules Jun 2026) do NOT alter the 200% MCE gross limit or EBT issuance/spend mechanics. AD-LIB BURN RISKS: (1) don't say the card buys 'anything' — no hot/prepared foods, alcohol, non-food, and no delivery fees online; 'groceries' as scripted is safe; (2) 'stores where you already shop' — CDSS says MOST grocery stores, not all; fine as scripted, don't promise a specific small store; (3) don't promise a specific benefit amount from these income figures — these are eligibility screens, not benefit levels; (4) figures roll over Oct 1, 2026 (FFY2027 COLA) — fine for July/Aug 2026 air, re-verify if episode re-airs after Oct 1; (5) monthly reload lands on a staggered date in the first 10 days of the month, not necessarily the 1st.

All four assertions survived adversarial refutation against live primary sources (fetched 2026-07-01). (a) SSI cash-out: CDSS 'CalFresh Food Benefits for SSI/SSP Recipients' page (cdss.ca.gov/inforesources/calfresh/supplemental-security-income) confirms 'Beginning June 1, 2019' AB 1811 reversed the cash-out policy; SSI/SSP recipients can receive both, other eligibility criteria permitting. 'For decades' holds — cash-out dated to SSI's 1974 start (~45 years). CDSS H.R.1 & CalFresh FAQ shows NO 2026 change to SSI eligibility, so the claim is still true at the July 20 record date. (b1) No gross-income test: 7 CFR 273.9(a) (verified via law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/7/273.9): 'Households which contain an elderly or disabled member shall meet the net income eligibility standards' — exempt from the gross test, net 100% FPL only. Script's 'what's left after key bills, the net number' is a fair plain-language rendering. (b2) Medical deduction: 7 CFR 273.9(d)(3): 'That portion of medical expenses in excess of $35 per month... incurred by any household member who is elderly or disabled' is deductible → raises the benefit. Still $35 in FY2026; H.R.1 did not touch it (and CA's Standard Medical Deduction pilot only makes it MORE generous). (b3) Separate household: 7 CFR 273.1(b)(2) confirms 60+, unable to purchase AND prepare meals due to a PERMANENT disability, counted (with spouse) as a separate household — but NOT granted 'when the income of the others with whom the elderly disabled individual resides (excluding the... individual and his or her spouse) exceeds 165 percent of the poverty line.' Script's 'sometimes' hedge is SUFFICIENT — the sentence promises nothing unconditional, matches the reg's shape (60+, disability, can't shop or cook, living with family), and low burn risk. Two residual nits, neither rising to needs_fix: (i) the reg requires a PERMANENT disability ('can't shop or cook... because of a disability' is slightly broader); (ii) the 165%-FPL cap on the family's income is the unstated condition behind 'sometimes.' Bonus: H.R.1's Nov 2025 utility-allowance cut spared elderly/disabled households (they keep the $20.01 energy-payment SUA route), so the senior-friendly framing is if anything understated.
result.scriptFix: No change required. Optional tightening if the host wants belt-and-suspenders: after '...even while living with family,' add 'it depends on the rest of the family's income, so ask the county when you apply.' If the segment allows one more word, 'because of a permanent disability' tracks the regulation more exactly than 'because of a disability.'

result.notes: Verified 2026-07-01 against foodbankofnc.org (live HTML, not just summaries) + official 211/iCarol record (icarol.info org=2265 agencynum=4662835, verified 2/19/2026 by FBNC ED Brett Benley). (1) NO-PAPERWORK: CONFIRMED — FBNC's own distributions page states verbatim 'No paperwork is required to receive food for your household.' The Alternate Pick-Up Form applies ONLY when collecting for a DIFFERENT household (3-household limit per car), and the USDA-income-guidelines note (TEFAP linkage) imposes no point-of-service means test ('No application required' per 211). Script's 'NO paperwork at all. Nothing. No forms, no income questions' is true for a viewer getting food for their own household — burn risk LOW, no change needed. (2) SCHEDULE: Grass Valley SDA 2nd & 4th Thu 10am-1pm CONFIRMED (live calendar + iCarol; note their Jul 9 calendar entry is mislabeled '2nd and 4th Tuesday' — Jul 9 is a Thursday, site data-entry slip). Truckee every Tue 4-5pm CONFIRMED (live calendar, events through Jul 7). North San Juan (2nd Tue 10-12, Oak Tree School), Washington (3rd Wed 12pm, Fire Dept), Higgins Corner (3rd Thu 3:30-5:30) CONFIRMED on iCarol and consistent with FBNC Programs page ('seven times each month at six locations' — 6th location unidentified; iCarol lists 5). Caveat: FBNC's live calendar widget is a rolling ~2-week window ending Jul 9, so the mid-July NSJ/Washington/Higgins events are not yet visible on the site itself — keep the planned re-verify call (530-272-3796) the week of Jul 20; 'plus more sites from North San Juan out to Washington' is safe as phrased (no days/times claimed on-air for those sites). (3) FREED: CONFIRMED — Programs page: home delivery 'In partnership with FREED Center for Independent Living,' twice each month, for 'homebound seniors, individuals with disabilities, and others who are unable to attend food distributions.' Script phrasing accurate (claims no frequency). (4) SUMMER LUNCH: CONFIRMED verbatim on homepage — 'June 15th-July 31st Summer Lunch 2026,' ages 2-18, Monday-Friday, 'No paperwork or sign-up needed, just show up!', children need not live at the apartment complex; 9 sites in 3 time bands (12:15-12:30 Grass Valley Terrace/Berryhill/Crown Point/Brunswick Common; 12:40-12:55 Oak Ridge/Valley Commons/Cashin's Field/Courtyard at Penn Valley; 1:05-1:20 Nevada Woods — note Oak Ridge/Nevada Woods time bands shifted vs the Feb research brief; script names no sites so no on-air impact, but the program card points viewers to the site, which is correct). THE FIX: the script's 'Right now, through about the end of July' is accurate at record (Jul 20) and air (~Jul 23-27) but the program ends Jul 31 — days after air — and the episode replays on YouTube indefinitely. 'Right now' goes stale immediately after July; reframe evergreen (see scriptFix). This is the only change needed. (5) ~2,500 CALFRESH: CONFIRMED verbatim on homepage today — 'Recent federal legislation, known as HR1... approximately 2,500 Nevada County residents have lost CalFresh benefits.' Script's attribution ('our local food bank says') and rounding ('about twenty-five hundred') both correct; wording 'grocery benefits' is fair plain-language for CalFresh/SNAP. PROGRAM CARD: phone 530-272-3796 and foodbankofnc.org both confirmed on the site.
result.scriptFix: Replace the Summer Lunch line (b) with an evergreen framing, e.g.: 'And every summer, the Food Bank runs a free summer lunch program — any child age two to eighteen, weekdays, at sites all around the county. No registration, no paperwork, no income anything. Kids just show up and eat. This year it runs through the end of July, and it's back every summer — current sites are always at foodbankofnc.org.' (Keeps the just-show-up hook, stays true on YouTube replays after July 31, and routes viewers to the live site list.) No other changes: claims (a), (c), and the program card are accurate as written.

result.notes: Verified 2026-07-01 directly against interfaithfoodministry.org (Home, Need Food?, How To Get Food, FAQ, Contact, Online Reservations pages; archive.org blocked from this env). Item-by-item: (1) ADDRESS+PHONE — ACCURATE: '440 Henderson Street, Grass Valley, CA 95945' (FAQ + Contact) and '(530) 273-8132' (Contact + How To Get Food). Program card is correct. (2) NO INCOME TEST — SUPPORTED: no IFM page mentions income anywhere; their complete requirements list is only (a) photo ID, (b) proof of Nevada County residency, (c) agree to Good Neighbor Requirements. FAQ eligibility: 'Anyone living in Nevada County who needs food assistance.' A search-indexed snippet of a now-404 IFM page (need-help.html) said food is available 'regardless of income' — IFM's own historical language. The 211 'income-based' summary is contradicted by IFM's own site; 'There is NO income test. Full stop.' is defensible on-air. Still on the week-of-July-20 phone re-verify list as a belt-and-suspenders. (3) ANY-NEVADA-COUNTY-RESIDENT — ACCURATE (FAQ verbatim above; a cached IFM snippet even mentions serving some border communities, so the script under-claims if anything). (4) DOCUMENTATION — ACCURATE: 'photo i.d. and proof of Nevada County residency (for example a bill, bank statement, or rental lease)' (FAQ); How To Get Food adds rental agreement/mortgage/current utility bill. Script's 'a bill or a lease' matches. Omits the Good Neighbor Requirements agreement — fine to leave out. (5) FREQUENCY — ACCURATE verbatim: 'You may pick up your food once EVERY OTHER week' (FAQ + Need Food). (6) M/W/F 10-1 — TRUE BUT INCOMPLETE → FIX: all pages confirm Mon/Wed/Fri 10am-1pm, BUT 10-11am is RESERVATIONS ONLY; walk-ins are 11am-1pm ('Reservations only 10a-11a, first come first served 11a-1p' — FAQ/Need Food/Home). As written, a viewer walking up at 10am gets turned to the back of the process. Also Good Neighbor rule: 'Please do not arrive before 9:30am.' Reserve online at portal.soxbox.co/pantry/interfaith-food-ministry or by phone; IFM says reservations cut waits to ~15-25 min vs 45-60+. (7) SATURDAYS — ACCURATE + still happening: every page says Sat 10am-1pm, no reservations (the 211 '10-12' listing is contradicted by IFM's own site 4x). Script's bare 'plus Saturdays' is safe. Note: IFM closes on holiday Saturdays (was closed Sat July 4) — not relevant to on-air copy. (8) UNHOUSED — ACCURATE verbatim: 'If you don't have a residence, you're still eligible, just let us know at check-in' (Need Food + How To Get Food). (9) CHOICE PANTRY / 'you pick your own groceries' — OVERSTATED → FIX: IFM's site never uses 'choice pantry,' and the Online Reservations page describes a DRIVE-THROUGH: traffic control checks you in, at the checkers station you 'select the ask fors of the day,' then loaders 'load your order that will either be packed already or close to being finished' into your car. So clients DO get choices among offered items, but they do not walk shelves picking groceries — much of the order is pre-packed. 'You pick your own groceries' will set the wrong picture. Food list itself is accurate: 'a variety of fresh produce, dairy, pantry staples, baked goods, and proteins when available' (FAQ).
result.scriptFix: Replace the choice-pantry sentence and the hours sentence. SUGGESTED: '...the Interfaith Food Ministry, on Henderson Street in Grass Valley. It works like a drive-through: you pull up, tell the volunteers your picks of the day, and they load your car -- fresh produce, dairy, pantry staples. If you live anywhere in Nevada County and you need food, you qualify. Full stop. There is NO income test. Bring a photo ID and something with your address -- a bill or a lease -- and you can shop once every other week. They're open Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from ten to one -- heads up, the first hour is by reservation, walk-ins start at eleven -- plus Saturdays, no reservation needed.' (Keep the unhoused line as-is; it matches IFM's wording. Program card is accurate as-is; optionally append 'first hour M/W/F by reservation' and IFM's reservation line 530-273-8132.)

result.notes: REFUTED AS SCRIPTED — and stronger than 'unconfirmed': affirmative live evidence says Market Match matching is NOT currently active at the Nevada City market. (1) ACTIVE-IN-JULY-2026: The Ecology Center — the statewide Market Match administrator — publishes the live dataset behind its official market finder (ecologycenter.org/wp-content/uploads/fm-finder/markets.json, data version 2026-06-28, three days old). It lists 'Nevada City Farmers Market – Summer' (Union Alley, Saturdays 8:30am–1pm, in season) with offers_calfresh_ebt: TRUE but offers_market_match: FALSE, match cap null, match season null. The field is actively maintained, not a data gap: 300 of 677 sites statewide are flagged market_match=true with populated caps/seasons, including 19 in neighboring counties (Placer/Sacramento/Yuba); EVERY Nevada County site is false. Corroborating: the last documented county funding cycle ran Sept 6 2025 – Feb 28 2026 'or until funds depleted' (nevadacountyca.gov CivicAlert AID=8360 via search extraction; hoodline 2025-09; KNCO 2025-09-05), and exhaustive searching (county CivicAlerts, yubanet, theunion, KNCO, hoodline, marketmatch.org, Sierra Harvest) found NO 2026–27 renewal announcement as of 2026-07-01. sierraharvest.org is behind a Sucuri challenge (HTTP 307, unfetchable) with no 2026 archive.org snapshot (latest: May 2024), so the local manager's page could not be read — but the administrator-of-record's own finder saying 'no match at Nevada City' outweighs it. Note the 2025-26 cycle STARTED in September; if 2026-27 follows that pattern, matching will still be off in late July when the episode airs. (2) CAP: current-documented figure is $10/visit (2025-26 cycle); $15 was the 2023 cap — script's $10 is the right number IF the program returns, but attribute it ('last season') in any hedge. (3) MECHANICS: EBT card + photo ID at the Market Manager's booth, buy tokens, receive matching tokens — confirmed as the standard MM mechanics (marketmatch.org, county announcements); accurate whenever the program runs. Plain EBT acceptance at the Nevada City Saturday market IS confirmed live (Ecology Center finder offers_calfresh_ebt=true; CropCart 2026 guide). (4) GRASS VALLEY: gvfarmersmarket.org fetched live 2026-07-01 still says EBT and Market Match are 'on hold while we reauthorize the market through the USDA' — the script's omission of GV remains correct. (The separate 'Market at Grass Valley' at Pine Creek shows EBT=true in the finder but no Market Match; script doesn't name it, no issue.)
result.scriptFix: OPTION A — HEDGED LINE (if the beat stays): 'Your CalFresh card works at the Nevada City farmers market on Saturdays. And here's a tip: ask at the market manager's booth whether Market Match is running this season — when it is, they match what you spend with up to ten extra dollars a visit, free, to spend on fresh fruits and vegetables. Last season the matching money ran through the end of February, so it always pays to ask.' OPTION B — PRODUCER RECOMMENDATION (preferred): Cut the definite Market Match beat. As of July 1 the statewide administrator's own finder shows matching OFF at Nevada City (EBT on), the last funding cycle ended Feb 28 2026 with no renewal announced, and the previous cycle didn't launch until September — so odds are the claim is false at both record (Jul 20) and air (late July). Replace with the confirmed, evergreen version: 'Your CalFresh EBT card is accepted at the Nevada City farmers market — Saturdays, downtown' + the Option A ask-at-the-booth hedge if you want to keep the Market Match tease. ACTION ITEM: have production call Sierra Harvest (local MM manager) or Nevada County DSS CalFresh before the July 20 record; if they confirm a live 2026-27 cycle, restore the original beat verbatim — the $10 cap and booth mechanics check out — citing whatever cap they state for the new cycle (it has changed year to year: $15 in 2023, $10 in 2025-26).

result.notes: Verified 2026-07-01 against goldcountryservices.org, sierraseniors.org, and agencyonaging4.org (live fetches). CONFIRMED — (1) Provider split: Area 4 AAA Nevada County roster lists Gold Country Community Services, Inc. (dba Gold Country Senior Services) = Western Nevada County, Sierra Senior Services = Eastern Nevada County (Truckee/North Tahoe); both providers' own service-area lists match. (2) Phones: (530) 446-6853 is GCSS's own MoW enrollment line (correct number for west-county meal signup; AAA lists the senior-center main line 273-4961 instead — both valid); 530-550-7600 confirmed on sierraseniors.org and AAA roster. (3) Donations: GCSS $4/meal suggested, with explicit wording 'no eligible senior will be denied a meal because of their inability to contribute'; SSS 'a voluntary contribution of $6 per meal is welcomed', 'contribute as they are able'. 'About four to six dollars' range is accurate. 'Nobody is turned away' is explicit on GCSS's site; SSS's site says voluntary/as-able but never states turn-away wording — substantively safe (both are OAA Title III-C programs, which bar denial for non-contribution), just not both providers' literal wording. (4) Congregate lunch: Sierra Gold Community Senior Center, 231 Colfax Ave, Grass Valley; lunch Mon/Tue/Thu at noon; anyone 60+ 'encouraged to attend'; suggested $4.00/day (60+), under-60 guests $10.00/meal; 'on your first visit... you will be asked to complete a participant information form'. All match the script. No reservation requirement stated anywhere on the provider site, so 'just show up' is consistent with their own wording — low burn risk; note no meals on holidays or when roads are unsafe. (5) Delivery days confirmed for ad-libs: west = Tue & Thu, 10am–1pm (no weekends/holidays); east = Mon–Fri, frozen weekend/holiday meals on request. THE FIX — eligibility framing over-promises for Truckee: the script's 'if cooking or shopping has gotten hard' matches GCSS's own standard ('unable to prepare meals and/or have difficulty shopping'), but Sierra Senior Services' home delivery requires being 'primarily homebound (leaves only for necessities)', lacking 2+ ADLs, and a medical referral. A Truckee senior who still gets out but finds cooking hard would likely be told they don't qualify for delivery (SSS offers a congregate dining alternative for exactly that case) — the show's signature 'wait, I don't qualify?' burn. Secondary (cheap insurance): add the lunch-café phone (530) 273-4961 to the program card, and prefer the full name 'Sierra Gold Community Senior Center'.
result.scriptFix: Keep everything as written EXCEPT hedge the Truckee eligibility and beef up the card. Script line: 'If cooking or shopping has gotten hard, Meals on Wheels delivers real meals to your door — Gold Country Senior Services covers the west side of the county. In Truckee, Sierra Senior Services delivers to seniors who mostly can't get out of the house — and if that's not quite you, they'll set you up with a group meal instead. Either way, one phone call and they'll figure out what fits.' (Donation and 'nobody turned away' lines stay as-is.) Program card: 'Gold Country Senior Services 530-446-6853 (west county) / Sierra Senior Services 530-550-7600 (Truckee) — Senior lunch: Sierra Gold Community Senior Center, 231 Colfax Ave, Grass Valley, Mon/Tue/Thu at noon — (530) 273-4961'.

result.notes: All five items verified against LIVE primary sources on 2026-07-01. The county WIC page's 403 was bot-blocking only — a browser-UA curl returned HTTP 200 and the live page matches the 2026-05-05 archive.org snapshot. (1) LOCAL AGENCY: live nevadacountyca.gov/867 is headed 'Nevada County Public Health WIC Program' and 'serves' both western county and Truckee — confirmed. (2) PHONES: Grass Valley 530-265-1454 confirmed by THREE sources (live county page, live state WIC clinic locator API at myfamily.wic.ca.gov/api/ClinicAPI, YubaNet county press release). Truckee 530-582-7814 confirmed by the live county page ('Call for a phone or office appointment at 530-265-1454 (Grass Valley) or 530-582-7814 (Truckee)') and YubaNet — HOWEVER the state locator API lists the Truckee clinic participant line as 530-582-7817 (7814 is the Eastern County Public Health front desk, which the county itself directs WIC applicants to). Airing 7814 is defensible and county-endorsed; during the pre-record confirmation call, ask which Truckee number they prefer on screen. (3) ADDRESSES: Grass Valley = Brighton Greens Resource Center, 988 McCourtney Road, Grass Valley, CA 95949 — ZIP is 95949 (NOT 95945), confirmed by both the live county contact block and the state locator API. Truckee = Joseph Government Center, 10075 Levon Avenue SUITE 202, Truckee, CA 96161 — confirmed by both sources; add 'Suite 202' if the address goes on screen. (4) APPOINTMENT MODES: live county page: 'WIC services are available by phone, video chat, and at two locations' + 'Call for a phone or office appointment'; YubaNet: 'appointments and services onsite, by phone, and video' — all three modes confirmed. 'Appointment, not a waiting line' is accurate; state locator shows Truckee is 'By appt only' Mon/Wed/Thu with full clinic days Tue/Fri 8-5 (closed 12-1), Grass Valley M-F 8-5 — so do NOT imply Truckee walk-in service. (5) URL: myfamily.wic.ca.gov confirmed live as the official CA WIC consumer site (eligibility prescreen + office locator + how-to-apply) — correct card URL. Bonus: county also offers an online interest form ('Express your interest... using our online form! A WIC staff member will follow up').
result.scriptFix: None required — script and card language air as-is. Optional hardening: (a) if the Truckee address appears on screen, render it as '10075 Levon Ave, Suite 202 (Joseph Government Center)'; (b) Grass Valley ZIP on any graphic is 95949; (c) at the already-planned pre-record confirmation call (530-265-1454), also ask whether Truckee callers should use 530-582-7814 (county-published, Public Health front desk) or 530-582-7817 (state-locator direct clinic line) — keep 7814 unless the office says otherwise.

result.notes: Adversarial check against LIVE primary sources, 2026-07-01. (1) CVB AMOUNTS CONFIRMED on live https://myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/FruitsVegBenefits: '$26 per month for children ages 1 to 5', '$48 per month for pregnant and postpartum individuals', '$52 per month for fully and partially breastfeeding individuals' ($78 if breastfeeding multiples), 'effective April 2026'. The stale $47-pregnant snippet is REFUTED — $47 was the old FY2025 BREASTFEEDING figure (USDA FY25 CVV/B page: $24/$43/$47, effective Oct 1 2024–Sep 30 2025); live CA page and USDA FY26 Policy Memo #2026-2 both say $48 pregnant/postpartum. Use $48. (2) PER CHILD CONFIRMED: the CVB is part of each participant's individual food package — USDA's own CVB history is denominated 'per month per child' and multi-source corroboration states each enrolled child receives their own individual benefit; CA 'How Can I Get WIC?' says 'all qualifying members of the family' receive WIC food benefits. Two kids enrolled = 2 × $26. One precision note: the $26 is for children AGES 1–5; infants under 1 get formula/baby food instead of produce money — the script's adjacent 'baby formula, baby food' line keeps this honest, but hosts shouldn't imply a newborn gets the $26. (3) FRESH/FROZEN/CANNED CONFIRMED (live page: 'dried fruit, fresh, canned, and frozen fruits and vegetables', organic included); script's omission of 'dried' is a fine simplification. (4) TIMING SAFE FOR RECORD+AIR: FY26 federal amounts effective Oct 1 2025–Sep 30 2026 (CA card reflects the April 2026 adjustment); July 20 record and late-July air are inside the window. ⚠️ REPLAY CAVEAT FOR PRODUCERS: FY27 adjustment hits Oct 1 2026, and the FY27 federal budget proposals would CUT the fruit/veg benefit substantially (CBPP/NWA coverage) — dollar figures MUST be re-verified before any YouTube replay or re-air after Oct 1 2026; consider an on-screen 'amounts as of mid-2026' chyron on the numbers segment. (5) FREE SUPPORT CONFIRMED: USDA (fna.usda.gov/wic): WIC provides groceries, support and more 'all at no cost to you'; CA WIC home lists nutrition education ('expert tips from professionals who specialize in nutrition') and breastfeeding support ('support and information about breastfeeding your baby from experts') as included services. No fees anywhere in WIC. (6) ENROLL-EARLY FRAMING CONFIRMED: national WIC outreach (signupwic.com) says get started 'as soon as you know you're pregnant'; pregnancy eligibility ends at fixed post-birth points (6 months postpartum, up to 1 year breastfeeding; kids to age 5), so earlier enrollment strictly yields more months of benefits — the script's causal framing is sound. Also spot-checked the staples list (milk, eggs, cereal, beans, baby formula, baby food) — matches USDA's 'fruits and vegetables, baby food, milk, eggs, beans, cheese, and more' and CA's WIC Card food list. Sources: myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/FruitsVegBenefits (live fetch), myfamily.wic.ca.gov/ (live fetch), myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/HowCanIGetWIC (live fetch), myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/AmIEligible (live fetch), fna.usda.gov/wic (USDA — note fns.usda.gov is migrating to fna.usda.gov; old URLs time out), USDA WIC Policy Memo #2026-2 (FY26 CVV/B), signupwic.com.
result.scriptFix: No fix required — all figures and framings verified against live primary sources. Optional precision tweak if hosts want it bulletproof: 'twenty-six dollars a month for each kid ages one to five' (infants under 1 get formula and baby food instead of the produce money). Producer action, not script: re-verify $26/$48/$52 before any replay after Oct 1, 2026 (FY27 adjustment + proposed federal CVB cuts).

result.notes: All three claims verified 2026-07-01 against LIVE primary sources; two independent primary sources agree on every number. (1) CAREGIVER CLAIM — CONFIRMED verbatim on live https://myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/AmIEligible: 'I have a child under age five (including foster children and those cared for by fathers or grandparents)'. Script's 'dads, grandparents, foster parents... YOU can enroll that child' is exactly the right framing (the child is the participant; the caregiver enrolls them). (2) INCOME FIGURES — CONFIRMED on both the live AmIEligible page AND the CDPH WPPM #980-1060 PDF (fetched directly this pass; the 'TLS error' is cdph.ca.gov serving an incomplete cert chain — content retrieved and matches the live page exactly). Effective May 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027 (both sources), so figures are STABLE across the July 20 record date. HH1 = $29,526; script says 'about twenty-nine thousand five hundred' = $29,500, which is $26 BELOW the real limit — rounding DOWN, conservative-safe, does NOT overstate reach. No fix needed. HH2 = $40,034; 'about forty thousand' is $34 below — conservative-safe. HH4 = $61,050; 'about sixty-one thousand' is $50 below — conservative-safe. 'Single parent + one child = household of TWO' is correct against the HH2 row. Bonus (unclaimed in script, from PDF footnote): a pregnant applicant may optionally count the unborn in household size. (3) POSTPARTUM WINDOW — page: 'I just had a baby (or had been pregnant) within the last six months' matches the script's six-month line; breastfeeding ('an infant less than 12 months old') is listed as a SEPARATE category in the script ('breastfeeding parents'), so the 12-month breastfeeding window is not undersold — a breastfeeding parent at month 8 correctly hears they qualify. Minor precision note (no fix required): script doesn't state the <12-month cap on the breastfeeding category, so a parent nursing a 13+ month-old could over-hear their OWN eligibility — but their child under 5 still qualifies, so the call-to-action outcome (make the appointment) is correct either way. (4) ADJUNCTIVE LIST — page confirms 'Medi-Cal, CalFresh, CalWORKs, and/or FDPIR' as automatic income-qualification. Script lists 3 of 4 (omits FDPIR) — a conservative omission, fine for this audience. Medi-Cal-card-as-proof CONFIRMED verbatim on https://www.myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/HowCanIGetWIC: 'If you or your child receive Medi-Cal, you may use your active Medi-Cal card as proof of income.' (5) BURN-RISK on 'ALREADY passed the income test. Automatically.' — SAFE AS WRITTEN: the script scopes the claim to the INCOME test only (which is exactly what adjunctive eligibility waives), not to enrollment, and the very next beat says 'bring your Medi-Cal card to the appointment,' which presupposes the remaining enrollment step. Primary source confirms the residual steps the script correctly leaves intact: 'Only staff at your local WIC office can officially confirm that you qualify for WIC services' and 'A nutrition assessment will also be completed' (AmIEligible + HowCanIGetWIC). No claim of auto-enrollment is made or reasonably implied.
result.scriptFix: None required. Optional belt-and-suspenders (host ad-lib, not a correction): after 'it doubles as your proof of income,' add '— you still make the one appointment; the income part is just already done.' This forecloses any residual 'I'm auto-enrolled' misreading, though the existing 'bring your Medi-Cal card to the appointment' line already implies it.
